theme_archetype.primary: Archetype 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast) mixed with Archetype 5 (Jewel & Luxury)
theme_archetype.rationale: For a premium fintech application, we want the authority and clarity of Swiss design combined with the high-end, cinematic feel of Jewel & Luxury. This ensures the app feels trustworthy yet modern and 'cool'.

font_family.headings: System's bold sans-serif (e.g. San Francisco on iOS, Roboto on Android) or Inter if using custom fonts. Must be geometric and clean.
font_family.body: System's regular sans-serif (e.g. San Francisco, Roboto) or Inter.
font_family.mono: Space Mono or System Mono for numbers/currency rates.

rules: No generic centering; left-align text where possible, center-align the main currency amount for dramatic focus.
Use tabular nums (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums) for all currency displays to avoid horizontal shifting.
Use extremely generous spacing (padding 6 or 8) around main converter cards to let the content breathe.
Containers must use soft inner shadows and very subtle borders (1px border-border) to define edges without heavy reliance on drop shadows.

main_converter_screen.layout: Vertical Stack: Header (Menu/Theme toggle) -> Source Currency Card -> Switch Button -> Target Currency Card -> Exchange Rate Info -> Keypad.
main_converter_screen.source_card: Large, transparent or glassmorphism block. Features country flag (rounded-full, 32x32), Currency Code (USD), and a massive text input/display for the amount.
main_converter_screen.target_card: Similar to source but visually distinct (e.g., slightly dimmer text or different background tint) to denote the 'result' state.
main_converter_screen.switch_icon: Absolute positioned circular button overlapping the boundary between source and target cards. Uses an up/down arrow icon.
main_converter_screen.exchange_rate_text: Small, centered under the cards. Includes 'Updated DD-MM-YYYY' and a tiny 'sync' icon indicating offline/online status.

custom_keypad.buttons: Large touch targets. Numbers (0-9), Decimal (.), Backspace, and 'C' or 'CE'.
custom_keypad.visuals: No visible button borders by default; solid background on active press. Backspace and CE should use accent_vibrant color or muted text to differentiate from numbers.
custom_keypad.interaction: Visual haptic feedback (scale down to 0.95 and reduce opacity on press).

currency_picker_modal.presentation: Bottom sheet or full-screen modal with a sleek search bar at the top.
currency_picker_modal.list_item: Row containing Flag (squircle), Currency Code (bold), Country Name (muted), and a 'Pin' (star) icon on the far right.
currency_picker_modal.pinned_section: Show 'Favorites' at the top of the list.

chart_panel.usage: Swipe left on a currency card or dedicated tab to view historical data.
chart_panel.visuals: Minimalist line chart using react-native-chart-kit or victory-native. No grid lines, just the trend curve and an area gradient underneath. X-axis shows 7D, 1M toggle.

multi_currency_comparison.layout: List view where the top is a sticky header showing the base amount (e.g., 100 USD), and below is a scrollable list of favorite currencies with their equivalent values.

animations_and_interactions.keypad_press: Use React Native Reanimated or standard Animated for a snappy scale-down (to 0.95) and spring back on release.
animations_and_interactions.list_transitions: Staggered entrance for the multi-currency list items.
animations_and_interactions.haptics: Trigger actual device haptics (Haptics.impactAsync) on keypad press and currency switch.

media_urls.flags: Use a public API for flags like 'https://flagcdn.com/' appending the 2-letter country code (e.g., https://flagcdn.com/w80/us.png). Instruct main agent to map currency codes to country codes for flags.

navigation_pattern.recommendation: Bottom Tab Navigation mixed with Stack.
navigation_pattern.modals: Use Stack modals (presentation: 'modal') for the Currency Picker.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is an Expo React Native application. Use NativeWind (Tailwind for React Native) for styling to adhere to these Tailwind classes where possible, or translate them to React Native StyleSheet.
Ensure the keypad is a custom component, do NOT use the native keyboard. Provide visual haptic feedback using TouchableOpacity or Pressable with animated scale.
For the UI, use a glassmorphism effect (blur view or translucent background) for the currency cards if the background has texture, otherwise use solid contrasting colors from the palette.
Store favorites and cached rates locally using AsyncStorage.
Add test IDs (`testID` in React Native, not `data-testid`) to all interactive elements for testing.
Use standard tabular-nums for text showing currency values to prevent jittering during conversion.
Handle dark and light modes using NativeWind's color scheme hook or a custom React context.
